title: Negundo sessilifolium
intro: Negundo sessilifolium, also known as Mexican buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Negundo sessilifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, sore throats, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Negundo sessilifolium is a plant species classified within the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the genus Negundo. The species is native to China. The classification of Negundo sessilifolium is as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: Angiosperms, clade: Eudicots, clade: Rosids, order: Sapindales, family: Sapindaceae, genus: Negundo, species: N. sessilifolium.
